text: Alright everyone, welcome back! Aaj hum BST Deletion cover karenge — yeh ek bahut important topic hai interviews ke liye. BST mein deletion thoda tricky hota hai kyunki hume BST property maintain karni hoti hai.
Before we start, quickly recall karte hain — BST mein left child hamesha parent se smaller hota hai, aur right child hamesha larger. This property must hold after every deletion.
BST deletion ke teen cases hote hain. Case 1: node is a leaf — it has no children. Case 2: node has one child. Case 3: node has two children. Sabse easy case 1 hai, sabse hard case 3.
Case 1 — Leaf node deletion. Agar node ka koi child nahi hai, simply usse remove kar do. Parent ka pointer null kar do. Bahut simple! O(h) time, jahan h is the height of the tree.
Case 2 — Node with one child. Agar node ka sirf ek child hai, to node ko bypass kar do. Parent ko directly us child se connect kar do. Like removing a link from a chain.
Example: Agar node 30 ko delete karna hai aur uska sirf ek right child 40 hai, to 30 ke parent ka right pointer 40 pe set kar do. BST property maintain rahegi kyunki 40 already 30 se bada tha.
Case 3 — Node with two children. Yeh sabse interesting case hai! Hum directly delete nahi kar sakte. Instead, hum in-order successor ya in-order predecessor dhundhte hain.

In-order successor kya hota hai? Yaad hai na — in-order traversal mein nodes sorted order mein aate hain. So in-order successor of a node is the next node in sorted order — i.e., smallest node in the right subtree.

Algorithm for case 3: Step 1 — Find in-order successor (leftmost node of right subtree). Step 2 — Copy successor's value to current node. Step 3 — Delete the successor from right subtree. Successor is always a leaf or has one child, so it's easier to delete!

Let's trace through an example. Consider deleting node 50 from a BST. Node 50 has left subtree with 30, 20, 40 and right subtree with 70, 60, 80. In-order successor of 50 is 60 (smallest in right subtree).
So hum 50 ki value ko 60 se replace karte hain, phir 60 ko right subtree se delete karte hain. 60 is a leaf node here, so case 1 applies. Final tree has 60 at the position where 50 was.

Time complexity of BST deletion: O(h) where h is height. For balanced BST, h = log n, so O(log n). For skewed BST, h = n, so O(n) worst case. This is why we use AVL Trees or Red-Black Trees in practice.
Let's write the code. deleteNode function lenge. Base case: agar root null hai, return null. Phir compare karo node ki value from target.
If target < root.val, recursively delete from left subtree: root.left = deleteNode(root.left, target). If target > root.val, delete from right subtree: root.right = deleteNode(root.right, target).
If target == root.val — yeh woh node hai jise delete karna hai. Case 1 check: if root.left == null and root.right == null, return null. Case 2 check: if only one child exists, return that child.
Case 3 code: find in-order successor. Traverse root.right ke left subtree mein jaao jab tak null na aaye. That's your successor. Copy its value to root. Then delete successor from root.right.

Pura code dekhte hain. deleteNode(root, key) { if (!root) return null; if (key < root.val) root.left = deleteNode(root.left, key); else if (key > root.val) root.right = deleteNode(root.right, key); else { ... } return root; }

The else block for case 3: let successor = root.right; while (successor.left) successor = successor.left; root.val = successor.val; root.right = deleteNode(root.right, successor.val); This elegantly handles all three cases.

Common interview question: Can you use in-order predecessor instead? Haan, bilkul! In-order predecessor is the largest node in left subtree. Both approaches are correct. Some implementations choose randomly.
Edge cases to remember: deleting root node, deleting from a tree with only one node (return null), deleting a node that doesn't exist (handle gracefully — just return tree unchanged).
Ab practice karte hain. Leetcode 450 — Delete Node in a BST. Yeh exactly yahi problem hai. Ek baar khud implement karo without looking at notes. 20 minute timer set karo.
Interviewer tip: Jab bhi BST problem aayi, first line mein bolo — 'I'll maintain the BST invariant: left < root < right'. Yeh shows you understand the core property. Big impression!
Related concepts jo aage aayenge — AVL Tree rotation after deletion, Red-Black Tree fixup. Yeh sab BST deletion pe based hain. Aaj jo seekha usse solid karo pehle.

Quick recap before we move on: 3 cases of BST deletion, in-order successor for case 3, O(h) time complexity. Notes bana lo ya mental model banao. Next class mein Tree Traversal — preorder, inorder, postorder — cover karenge.
Any doubts? Chat mein likhna. Sab questions valuable hain — 'basic' lagta ho ya na ho. Sheryians mein judging nahi hoti! See you in the next lecture. Keep coding!

Bonus: Interview story. Ek student ne Google interview mein exactly yeh BST Delete question pocha tha. Unhone in-order successor approach explain kiya clearly, whiteboard pe diagram banaya, aur got the offer! Confidence + clarity = success.

Recursive vs iterative: Jo code humne likha woh recursive hai. Iterative approach bhi possible hai using a parent pointer. Recursive is cleaner for interviews. Space complexity: O(h) stack frames for recursive, O(1) for iterative.
Practice problems for today: LeetCode 450 (BST Delete), LeetCode 700 (Search in BST), LeetCode 701 (Insert into BST). Yeh teen problems BST CRUD operations cover karte hain completely.

Tomorrow hum Tree Traversal cover karenge. Preorder (Root-Left-Right), Inorder (Left-Root-Right), Postorder (Left-Right-Root), and Level Order (BFS). Inorder on BST gives sorted output — very useful property!

Assignment for today: Implement BST Delete from scratch in your preferred language. Then dry-run on 5 different tree configurations. Especially test case 3 thoroughly with both left and right subtrees present.
